title: Exact Decimal/legal-units arithmetic and rounding foundation (DF-2)
objective: Replace binary-float legal math in the rule-evaluation core with Decimal/rational computed from canonical decimal strings; enforce per-rule rounding mode/scale/order and unit consistency; keep geometry floats isolated behind explicit typed conversions; add property-based, differential, and adversarial exact-threshold/rounding tests. Deterministic offline foundation that unblocks exact FAR/coverage/yard-boundary comparisons; publication-blocker foundation per DF-2 / B-014.
business_reason: D-002 first-wave lane 2 (Section 7.2); whole-system trust replan Area D / defect DF-2 / blocker B-014 (owner made exact legal arithmetic a mandatory M4 rule-publication blocker).
inputs: services/api/app/rules/operations.py, evaluator.py (current float math @ frozen base)
outputs: services/api/app/rules/evaluator.py (exact comparisons at thresholds; geometry-float isolation preserved)
services/api/app/rules/units.py (NEW: canonical decimal + unit-enforcement helpers; frozen interface)
services/api/tests/rules/** (property-based, differential native-vs-recompute, adversarial exact-threshold and rounding tests)
forbidden_paths: services/api/app/rules/rulesets/** (published rule CONTENT; no legal-semantics change without G6)
CLAUDE.md, .claude/**, .github/**, tools/**, render.yaml, any other task's paths or report
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 (executable): no binary-float arithmetic remains on the legal-value path in operations.py/evaluator.py (grep/AST check: legal numeric ops go through Decimal/rational; geometry floats remain isolated behind typed conversions).

AS-2 (executable): adversarial exact-threshold suite passes - FAR/coverage/yard equalities at representable-vs-non-representable decimals resolve deterministically (e.g. exact FAR == cap boundary) with no float representation error.
AS-3 (executable): differential test - native evaluator output equals an independent Decimal recompute across generated inputs; property-based invariants (scaling lot area scales FAR cap) hold.
AS-4 (executable): per-rule rounding mode/scale/order and unit enforcement are explicit and covered; non-finite/malformed decimals fail closed (never silently coerced).
AS-5: existing rules-engine tests still pass (no published legal-semantics change; only arithmetic exactness improved).
risks: Changing rounding could shift a draft rule's numeric output - mitigated: rules are draft/awaiting-G6 (not published), and legal SEMANTICS/rule content are out of scope (rulesets/** forbidden).
Geometry floats must stay isolated - mitigated by explicit typed-conversion boundary + tests.
